title: Update Deprecation Recommendation Filter to Exclusive Date Logic
story: As a Salesforce administrator, I want to filter reports by a 'not viewed in X days' condition, so that I can identify unused reports to recommend for deprecation.
description:
The existing date range filters operated on an inclusive basis (show reports viewed within the range). The deprecation recommendation feature requires an exclusive condition: show reports **not** viewed in the last N days.

### Logic
To find reports not viewed in the last 90 days:
- Calculate end date as `TODAY - 90 days`
- Set start date to a safe historical date (e.g., 2015-01-01)
- Filter for reports where `last_viewed` falls within this range (i.e., last viewed more than 90 days ago)

### Implementation
A new dropdown was added above the "Last Viewed Date" filter with an inclusive/exclusive condition toggle (`=` or `!=`). A help icon tooltip explains: *"This filter only applies if a date range is selected in the 'Last Viewed Date' field. Without a selected date range, changing this condition will have no impact on the displayed results."*
acceptance_criteria: A condition dropdown appears above the Last Viewed Date filter
Dropdown allows selecting inclusive (=) or exclusive (!=) conditions
Exclusive condition correctly filters reports NOT viewed within the selected date range
Help icon tooltip explains the filter dependency on date range selection
Default 90-day deprecation recommendation works correctly using the exclusive filter
ux_changes: Added a condition dropdown above the Last Viewed Date filter allowing inclusive/exclusive filtering. Help icon tooltip explains the filter behavior.
middleware_changes: Updated date filter logic to support exclusive (!=) conditions, calculating the date range for reports not viewed within the specified period.
implementation_notes: The exclusive condition inverts the typical date range filter. For a 90-day deprecation window, the system calculates TODAY - 90 days as the end date and uses a far historical start date to capture all reports not viewed recently. This required overriding the standard filter behavior since the existing filter configuration didn't support exclusive conditions natively.
comment_summary: Extended discussion about how to implement an exclusive date filter when the existing filter system only supports inclusive conditions. The solution involved adding a condition dropdown with inclusive/exclusive options and a help icon explaining the dependency on date range selection.
